M02-title.title: Module 02
M02-title.subtitle: Bone System (Bone)
M02-title.notes: This is the most core and information-dense module of the entire training. The Bone layer determines the persuasion logic of the video.

M02-why-important.title: Why Is This the Most Important Module?
M02-why-important.studentContent:
Bone = Decision-Making Core

Mastering the Bone layer = Mastering the core logic of content production

This module answers three key questions:

1. What is the objective of this video? (Objective)
2. What parts make up the video? (Skeleton)
3. What persuasion approach does each part use? (Strategy)
M02-why-important.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Tip: Emphasize that this is the most important module. Objective determines the video direction, Skeleton determines the structure, and Strategy determines the persuasion approach.
M02-why-important.notes: Emphasize the three core questions to set the tone for the entire module.

M02-objectives.title: Learning Objectives
M02-objectives.studentContent:
After completing this module, you will be able to:

  • Select the correct Objective (video goal) based on business needs
  • Identify and apply 7 Section types (HOOK/BRIDGE/PROBLEM/RESOLUTION/REVERSAL/VALUE/CTA)
  • Master 8 Skeleton frameworks (Tier A/B/C: Tier-A basic 4-segment / Tier-B 5-segment / Tier-C with Bridge)
  • Understand the Strategy L0/L1/L2 three-layer strategy system
  • Complete a full skeleton analysis of a video
M02-objectives.notes: 5 learning objectives covering Objective, Section, Skeleton, and Strategy.

M02-part1-title.title: Part 1
M02-part1-title.subtitle: What Is a Persuasion Path
M02-part1-title.notes: Establish the concept that "a video = a process of changing the viewer's mental state."

M02-1-question.title: What Is the Essence of a Video?
M02-1-question.studentContent:
What is a video essentially doing?

Delivering information? Showcasing products? Persuading users?
Video = A process of changing the viewer's mental state
M02-1-question.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Before we dive into the specifics, I want to ask everyone a question: What is a video essentially doing?

(Wait 2-3 seconds)

Some say "delivering information," some say "showcasing products," others say "persuading users." These are all correct, but I want to give you a more fundamental answer: A video is essentially a process of changing the viewer's mental state.
M02-1-question.notes: This metaphor is very important and serves as the cognitive foundation for the entire training.

M02-1-state-change.title: State A → State B
M02-1-state-change.studentContent:
State A (Before Watching)
  • ❌ Doesn't know who you are
  • ❌ Doesn't care what you're selling
  • ❌ Doesn't trust what you say
→
State B (After Watching)
  • ✅ Interested in the product
  • ✅ Believes what you say
  • ✅ Willing to take action
The path from State A to State B is the "Persuasion Path"
The Bone system is the tool for designing this persuasion path
M02-1-state-change.notes: Ensure students understand that "a video is a process" rather than "a collection of content pieces."

M02-part2-title.title: Part 2
M02-part2-title.subtitle: Objective: Video Goal
M02-part2-title.notes: This is a newly added key section! Before discussing the skeleton, you must first clarify the video objective.

M02-2-why-objective.title: Why Define the Objective First?
M02-2-why-objective.studentContent:
You can't make a good video if you don't know what it's supposed to achieve

Before starting to write a script, you must answer one question:

"What is the objective of this video?"

Different objective → Different structure → Different strategy → Different content
A follower-growth video and a sales-conversion video differ completely in both skeleton and strategy.
M02-2-why-objective.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Many content creators start writing scripts as soon as they receive a brief. That's wrong. The first step is always: clarify the objective of this video.

If the objective is wrong, everything you write afterward is wasted effort. The objective determines which skeleton you choose and which strategy you use.
M02-2-why-objective.notes: Emphasize the importance of objective-first thinking — this is something many content creators tend to overlook.

M02-2-objective-groups.title: Two Objective Groups (9 Objectives)
M02-2-objective-groups.notes: Two categories: Account Growth (3) vs Commerce (6). Descriptions from core-defs.

M02-2-objective-mix.title: ❓ Can You Combine Different Objectives?
M02-2-objective-mix.studentContent:
Principle: A video can only have one primary objective
But it can have secondary objectives as "bonus benefits"

🎯 Decision Principles:

  • The primary objective determines the skeleton and core strategy; secondary objectives are just "incidental"
  • If two objectives conflict (e.g., GROW vs CONVERT), you must choose one
  • The more focused the objective, the better the video performs

✅ Examples of compatible combinations:

SEED + PERSONA
A product seeding video that also showcases the creator's expertise
CONVERT + BRAND
A promotional video that reinforces brand memory
GROW + BOND
Follower-growth content that existing fans also want to share
❌ GROW + CONVERT
Follower growth and sales conversion are often opposing goals
M02-2-objective-mix.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Some of you might ask: Can a single video both grow followers and drive sales?

The answer is: You can only have one primary objective. Why? Because the content qualities needed for follower growth (entertaining, valuable, shareable) and those needed for sales (product showcase, benefit emphasis, purchase urgency) often conflict.

You can have secondary objectives, but the primary one must stay focused.
M02-2-objective-mix.notes: Address the common question: Can you combine multiple objectives? Emphasize the principle that the primary objective must be unique.

M02-part3-title.title: Part 3
M02-part3-title.subtitle: Section: Segment Types
M02-part3-title.notes: 7 segment types — essentially a psychological progression sequence.

M02-3-section-intro.title: What Is a Section?
M02-3-section-intro.studentContent:
Section = A stage of psychological progression
Not a "content type," but a "viewer state transition"

🔥 Core Concept: A Section is a psychological progression sequence, not a content sequence

Attention Capture → Relevance Building → Tension Creation → Tension Release → Decision Reinforcement → Action Trigger
M02-3-section-intro.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Many people think a Section is a "content category" — product info goes here, evidence goes there. That's wrong.

The essence of a Section is psychological progression. The task of each Section is to move the viewer from one psychological state to the next.
M02-3-section-intro.notes: The essence of a Section is psychological progression, not content categorization. This is the core concept!

M02-3-section-all.title: 7 Section Types
M02-3-section-all.studentContent:
Section Name Viewer State Transition Core Function
HOOK Attention Capture Scrolling → Attentive Interrupt auto-scrolling, compete for cognitive bandwidth ✅ Required
BRIDGE Relevance Building Attentive → Personally Relevant Establish context and personal relevance ⚠️ Highly Recommended
PROBLEM Tension Building Calm → Uneasy Expose the gap between reality and ideal, create motivation to change ⭐ Strongly Recommended
RESOLUTION Tension Release Uneasy → Hopeful Relieve tension, show a path to improvement ⭐ Strongly Recommended
REVERSAL Cognitive Reframe Certain → Rethinking Challenge existing assumptions, reshape understanding Not Required
VALUE Decision Reinforcement Interested → Convinced Provide reasons to choose, reduce perceived risk ⭐ Strongly Recommended
CTA Action Trigger Convinced → Acting Convert inclination into action ✅ Required
M02-3-section-all.presenterContent: 💡 Core Chain: HOOK (grab attention) → BRIDGE (build relevance) → PROBLEM (create tension) → RESOLUTION (release tension) → VALUE (reinforce decision) → CTA (trigger action)
M02-3-section-all.notes: The 7 Sections form a complete psychological progression chain.

M02-3-section-mantra.title: 🎯 Quick Identification Mnemonics
M02-3-section-mantra.studentContent:
😣 Uncomfortable
→ PROBLEM
Makes the viewer anxious, alert
😌 There's hope
→ RESOLUTION
Relieves anxiety, shows a way out
😲 I was wrong
→ REVERSAL
Beliefs overturned, rethinking triggered
🤑 Worth buying
→ VALUE
Provides reasons to choose
👆 Go do it
→ CTA
Guides immediate action
M02-3-section-mantra.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Remember these five phrases, and you'll be able to quickly identify which Section any piece of content belongs to:

Uncomfortable → PROBLEM
There's hope → RESOLUTION
I was wrong → REVERSAL
Worth buying → VALUE
Go do it → CTA
M02-3-section-mantra.notes: This mnemonic is the core tool for quick identification — make sure students memorize it.

M02-3-section-diff.title: ⚠️ Most Commonly Confused: RESOLUTION vs REVERSAL
M02-3-section-diff.studentContent:
RESOLUTION
What changes: The situation
"The problem can be solved"
The world becomes feasible
Example: "This product can block the melanin production pathway"
REVERSAL
What changes: The perception
"Your previous understanding was wrong"
The worldview gets rewritten
Example: "You thought it was dark spots, but it's actually yellowing caused by glycation"
Golden Test Question: Was the viewer wrong before?
YES → REVERSAL ｜ NO → RESOLUTION
M02-3-section-diff.notes: RESOLUTION changes the situation; REVERSAL changes the perception. This is the most common misidentification.

M02-part4-title.title: Part 4
M02-part4-title.subtitle: Skeleton Presets
M02-part4-title.notes: The 8 skeletons (Tier A/B/C) are standardized combinations of Sections.

M02-4-skeleton-intro.title: What Is a Skeleton?
M02-4-skeleton-intro.studentContent:
Skeleton = Preset Combination of Sections
A proven, standardized video structure template

Just like LEGO has "kits," a Skeleton is a video "kit":

  • No need to design the structure from scratch every time
  • Choose the right skeleton based on your objective and video length
  • Then fill in the content
M02-4-skeleton-intro.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Now that we have these Section "building blocks," the question is: how do we combine them?

The good news is that we don't need to start from zero every time. We've identified 8 standard skeletons, organized into three tiers (A/B/C) by complexity.
M02-4-skeleton-intro.notes: Skeleton is a "combo meal" of Sections, reducing decision costs.

M02-4-skeleton-all.title: 8 Skeleton Types (Tier A/B/C)
M02-4-skeleton-all.studentContent:
Abbreviations: H=HOOK, P=PROBLEM, Re=RESOLUTION, Rv=REVERSAL, V=VALUE, C=CTA, Br=BRIDGE
Tier A — Basic 4-Segment (Beginner-Friendly)
HPVCH → P → V → C4-seg Problem Type | 30-45s
HBVCH → Re → V → C4-seg Tension Release | 30-45s
HRVCH → Rv → V → C4-seg Reversal Type | 30-45s
Tier B — 5-Segment (Fuller Narrative)
HPRVCH → P → Rv → V → C5-seg Problem+Reversal | 45-60s
HPBVCH → P → Re → V → C5-seg Problem+Release | 45-60s
Tier C — With BRIDGE Setup (Requires More Control)
HBrPVCH → Br → P → V → C5-seg Bridge+Problem | 45-60s
HBrBVCH → Br → Re → V → C5-seg Bridge+Release | 50-60s
HBrPBVCH → Br → P → Re → V → C6-seg Full Bridge | 50-90s
M02-4-skeleton-all.presenterContent: 💡 Memory Tip: All skeletons start with H and end with C. Tier-A is 4-segment for beginners, Tier-B is 5-segment for richer narrative, Tier-C includes Bridge and requires stronger control. Start with Tier-A.
M02-4-skeleton-all.notes: Tier-A for beginners, Tier-B for fuller narrative, Tier-C with Bridge requires more control.

M02-4-skeleton-decision.title: 🌳 Skeleton Selection Decision Tree
M02-4-skeleton-decision.studentContent:
Pick a Tier first → Then choose the specific skeleton
Tier A — 30-45s, Quick Production
Need to pose problem → deliver value → HPVC (4-seg Problem Type)
Jump straight to solution → deliver value → HBVC (4-seg Tension Release)
Need cognitive reversal → deliver value → HRVC (4-seg Reversal Type)
Tier B — 45-60s, Fuller Narrative
Problem + reversal dual drive, high persuasion → HPRVC (5-seg Problem+Reversal)
Problem + tension release, standard narrative → HPBVC (5-seg Problem+Release)
Tier C — With BRIDGE, 50-90s
Large context gap, need to build prerequisites → HBrPVC / HBrBVC / HBrPBVC
💡 Beginner Tip: Start with Tier-A. HPVC (Problem Type) is the safest choice.
Tier-C's BRIDGE can slow pacing — only use when the context gap is large.
M02-4-skeleton-decision.notes: Select by tier level: beginners start with Tier-A, intermediate use Tier-B, Tier-C requires stronger control.

M02-part5-title.title: Part 5
M02-part5-title.subtitle: Strategy — Persuasion Tactics
M02-part5-title.notes: Strategy is the soul of the skeleton, determining "how to say it." This is the most detailed section — teach it progressively.

M02-5-intro.title: Skeleton vs Strategy
M02-5-intro.studentContent:
Skeleton
What parts make up the video
= "What to say"
+
Strategy
How each part persuades
= "How to say it"
Strategy is bound to each Section, determining the persuasion approach for that segment
M02-5-intro.notes: Clarify the distinction between Skeleton and Strategy.

M02-5-architecture.title: 🏗️ L0-L1-L2 Three-Layer Architecture Overview
M02-5-architecture.studentContent:
Strategy System = L0 Strategic Layer → L1 Tactical Layer → L2 Execution Layer (progressively refined)
🧠 HEAD
Rational Persuasion
EXPLAIN
Logic · Data · Transparency
PROOF
UGC · Before_After · Sales_Data
AUTHORITY
Expert · Certification · Media · Guarantee
CONTRAST
AB · Shocking · Value_Stack
❤️ HEART
Emotional Appeal
PAIN
Scene · Daily · Loss
FEAR
Consequence · Miss_Out
STORY
Origin · User
👁️ EYES
Seeing Is Believing
DEMO
Step · Multi_Angle
✋ HAND
Drive Action
SUSPENSE
Question · Reveal
URGENCY
Time · Stock
🔵 L0 = Main Headings ⬜ L1 = Card Titles 📝 L2 = Card Content 4 L0s → 10 L1s → 26 L2s
M02-5-architecture.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
Take a look at this diagram. Strategy is a three-layer cascading system.

The blue/red/purple/orange main headings at the top are L0 — there are only 4, representing the broad persuasion directions.

Under each L0, there are several L1s. For example, HEAD has four: EXPLAIN, PROOF, AUTHORITY, and CONTRAST.

Under each L1, there are several L2s — specific techniques you can use directly.

This is what "cascading" means: L0 contains L1, and L1 contains L2.
M02-5-architecture.notes: Show the clear hierarchy so students understand the cascading structure.

M02-5-full-table.title: 📊 Complete Strategy Reference Table
M02-5-full-table.studentContent:
L0 L1 L2 Specific Tactics
HEAD
Rational Persuasion		 EXPLAIN Logic (Logical Reasoning) · Data (Data Evidence) · Transparency (Ingredient Transparency)
PROOF UGC (User Testimonials) · Before_After (Before & After Comparison) · Sales_Data (Sales / Repurchase Data)
AUTHORITY Expert (Expert Endorsement) · Certification (Certifications & Awards) · Media (Media Coverage) · Guarantee (Promise & Assurance)
CONTRAST AB (A/B Comparison) · Shocking (Shocking Contrast) · Value_Stack (Value Stacking)
HEART
Emotional Appeal		 PAIN Scene (Scenario Resonance) · Daily (Daily Frustrations) · Loss (Loss Aversion)
FEAR Consequence (Consequence Warning) · Miss_Out (Fear of Missing Out)
STORY Origin (Brand Story) · User (User Story)
EYES
Seeing Is Believing		 DEMO Step (Step-by-Step Demo) · Multi_Angle (Multi-Angle Showcase)
HAND
Drive Action		 SUSPENSE Question (Question Hook) · Reveal (Reveal & Twist)
URGENCY Time (Flash Sale) · Stock (Limited Stock)
💡 Memory Tip: 4 L0s → 10 L1s → 26 L2s. L0 is the direction, L1 is the approach, L2 is the specific action.
M02-5-full-table.notes: Complete reference table for students. Can be printed as a quick-reference card.

M02-5-L0-overview.title: L0: Four Persuasion Paths
M02-5-L0-overview.studentContent:
🧠 HEAD

Cognition & Trust - Rational Persuasion

Core Question: "Why should I believe?"

"Let me explain the logic, and you'll be convinced"

❤️ HEART

Emotion & Resonance - Emotional Appeal

Core Question: "Why should I care?"

"I understand you — I've been there too"

👁️ EYES

Visual & Experience - Seeing Is Believing

Core Question: "How does it perform?"

"Look — it's that amazing"

✋ HAND

Attraction & Action - Drive Conversion

Core Question: "Why now?"

"Buy now or miss out forever"
M02-5-L0-overview.presenterContent: 💡 Teaching Tip: Have students memorize the 4 core questions: Why believe? Why care? How does it perform? Why now?
M02-5-L0-overview.notes: The four paths are the core of Strategy. Have students memorize the keywords and core questions.

M02-5-HEAD-title.title: 🧠 L0: HEAD — Cognition & Trust
M02-5-HEAD-title.studentContent:
🧠 HEAD
Core Logic: Persuade the viewer through reason
"Let me explain it clearly, and you'll believe what I'm saying is true"
Best Used For:
• Functional products (skincare, supplements)
• High-ticket products (need to build trust)
• Rational target audiences (professionals, educated consumers)
4 L1s Under HEAD:
EXPLAIN - Mechanism Explanation: Clarify "why it works"
PROOF - Real Evidence: Let facts and data speak
AUTHORITY - Authority Endorsement: Leverage credibility to build trust
CONTRAST - Contrast & Comparison: Highlight differences and advantages
M02-5-HEAD-title.notes: HEAD is the rational persuasion path, best suited for scenarios that require building trust.

M02-5-HEAD-EXPLAIN.title: HEAD → EXPLAIN — Mechanism Explanation
M02-5-HEAD-EXPLAIN.studentContent:
HEAD → EXPLAIN Mechanism Explanation Clarify "why it works"
Logic Logical Reasoning
Persuade through cause-and-effect logic chains
"Because it contains XX ingredient, it can XX, thus achieving XX effect"
Data Data Evidence
Use experimental/statistical data to support claims
"Tests show that after 28 days, XX metric improved by 37%"
Transparency Ingredient Transparency
Disclose ingredients/processes to build trust
"Our formula is fully public — you can check it yourself"
M02-5-HEAD-EXPLAIN.notes: EXPLAIN has 3 L2s: Logic/Data/Transparency. The core is "explain why clearly."

M02-5-HEAD-PROOF.title: HEAD → PROOF — Real Evidence
M02-5-HEAD-PROOF.studentContent:
HEAD → PROOF Real Evidence Let facts and data speak
UGC User Testimonials
Display real user feedback
"See what buyers are saying..." "User feedback: after 2 weeks..."
Before_After Before & After Comparison
Compare results before and after usage
"Before vs. After" "See the change in 28 days"
Sales_Data Sales / Repurchase Data
Use sales & repurchase data to prove popularity
"100K units sold in 3 days" "80% repurchase rate"
M02-5-HEAD-PROOF.notes: PROOF has 3 L2s: UGC/Before_After/Sales_Data. The core is "let facts speak."

M02-5-HEAD-AUTHORITY.title: HEAD → AUTHORITY — Authority Endorsement
M02-5-HEAD-AUTHORITY.studentContent:
HEAD → AUTHORITY Authority Endorsement Leverage credibility to build trust
Expert Expert Endorsement
Borrow authority from professionals
"Recommended by dermatologists" "Nutritionists swear by it" "Tested by XX expert"
Certification Certifications & Awards
Official certification boosts credibility
"FDA approved" "XX patent holder" "ISO quality certified"
Media Media Coverage
Leverage media credibility
"Featured by XX media" "Published in XX magazine" "Recommended on XX show"
Guarantee Promise & Assurance
Reduce perceived user risk
"7-day no-questions-asked returns" "Full refund if unsatisfied" "Free replacement for quality issues"
M02-5-HEAD-AUTHORITY.notes: AUTHORITY has 4 L2s: Expert/Certification/Media/Guarantee. The core is "leverage credibility."

M02-5-HEAD-CONTRAST.title: HEAD → CONTRAST — Contrast & Comparison
M02-5-HEAD-CONTRAST.studentContent:
HEAD → CONTRAST Contrast & Comparison Highlight differences and advantages
AB A/B Comparison
Direct comparison of similar products/solutions
"I compared it with XX big brand" "Left side is XX, right side is XX"
Shocking Shocking Contrast
Break expectations with dramatic contrast
"You'd never guess how big the gap is" "This result shocked me"
Value_Stack Value Stacking
Use gifts, bundles, price anchoring to exceed value expectations
"Buy one get three" "Was $99, now $29"
M02-5-HEAD-CONTRAST.notes: CONTRAST has 3 L2s: AB/Shocking/Value_Stack. The core is "contrast creates perceived value."

M02-5-HEART-title.title: ❤️ L0: HEART — Emotion & Resonance
M02-5-HEART-title.studentContent:
❤️ HEART
Core Logic: Move the viewer through emotion
"I understand how you feel — I've been there too, so you can trust me"
Best Used For:
• Emotion-related problems (anxiety, insecurity)
• Products with compelling stories
• Emotionally-driven target audiences
3 L1s Under HEART:
PAIN - Pain Point Resonance: Trigger the "you get me" feeling
FEAR - Fear Appeal: Create urgency and a sense of crisis
STORY - Story Narrative: Build emotional connection through stories
M02-5-HEART-title.notes: HEART is the emotional persuasion path, best suited for emotional audiences and products with stories.

M02-5-HEART-PAIN.title: HEART → PAIN — Pain Point Resonance
M02-5-HEART-PAIN.studentContent:
HEART → PAIN Pain Point Resonance Trigger the "you get me" feeling
Scene Scenario Resonance
Describe typical pain-point scenarios
"Ever had a breakout right before a date?" "Every morning, waking up with a puffy face"
Daily Daily Frustrations
List specific everyday annoyances
"Every morning waking up with a puffy face" "Oily skin by noon every day"
Loss Loss Aversion
Emphasize the cost of not solving the problem
"If you ignore this, your skin will only get worse" "Don't wait until it's too late"
M02-5-HEART-PAIN.notes: PAIN has 3 L2s: Scene/Daily/Loss. The core is "empathy."

M02-5-HEART-FEAR.title: HEART → FEAR Fear Appeal
M02-5-HEART-FEAR.studentContent:
HEART → FEAR Fear Appeal Create urgency and a sense of crisis
Consequence Consequence Warning
Show negative consequences of inaction
"If this continues long-term, it will lead to XX" "The consequence of doing this is..."
Miss_Out Fear of Missing Out
Create urgency about missing an opportunity
"This price is gone after today" "Don't miss out again"
⚠️ Note: Use FEAR in moderation; overuse will put people off. Best paired with RESOLUTION -- create anxiety first, then offer a solution.
M02-5-HEART-FEAR.notes: FEAR has 2 L2 tactics: Consequence/Miss_Out. The core idea is 'creating urgency,' but use it in moderation.

M02-5-HEART-STORY.title: HEART → STORY Storytelling
M02-5-HEART-STORY.studentContent:
HEART → STORY Storytelling Build emotional connection through stories
Origin Brand Story
Tell the brand/product origin story
"The founder of this brand originally wanted to help XX..." "It all started with..."
User User Story
Real user experiences and transformations
"A user told me that after using it, she..." "From skeptic to loyal fan"
M02-5-HEART-STORY.notes: STORY has 2 L2 tactics: Origin/User. The core idea is 'moving people through stories.'

M02-5-EYES-title.title: 👁️ L0: EYES Visual & Experience
M02-5-EYES-title.studentContent:
👁️ EYES
Core logic: Let the audience "see it to believe it"
"Don't take my word for it -- just look, the results speak for themselves"
Best for:
• Products with visible results (beauty, cleaning)
• Usage processes with visual impact
• Clear before-and-after differences
EYES has 1 L1:
DEMO - Demonstration: Show results directly through visuals
💡 Key trait: EYES is the most direct persuasion path. If the product delivers visible results, "seeing is believing" is more persuasive than any words.
M02-5-EYES-title.notes: EYES is the visual persuasion path, best suited for products with visible effects.

M02-5-EYES-DEMO.title: EYES → DEMO Demonstration
M02-5-EYES-DEMO.studentContent:
EYES → DEMO Demonstration Show results directly through visuals
Step Step-by-Step Demo
Show usage method step by step
"Step 1: First XX, Step 2: Then XX" "Here's the correct way to use it..."
Multi_Angle Multi-Angle Showcase
Show the product from multiple angles and scenarios
"From this angle, the effect is even more visible" "Look at it in different lighting"
M02-5-EYES-DEMO.notes: DEMO has 2 L2 tactics: Step/Multi_Angle. The core idea is 'seeing is believing.'

M02-5-HAND-title.title: ✋ L0: HAND Attraction & Action
M02-5-HAND-title.studentContent:
✋ HAND
Core logic: Push the audience to take action
"If you don't buy/watch now, you'll miss out/regret it"
Especially suited for:
• HOOK openings (use suspense to attract)
• CTA closings (use urgency to drive action)
• During promotional campaigns
HAND has 2 L1s:
SUSPENSE - Suspense & Questions: Spark curiosity, keep them watching
URGENCY - Scarcity & Urgency: Create urgency, push action
M02-5-HAND-title.notes: HAND is the action-oriented path, especially suited for HOOK and CTA.

M02-5-HAND-SUSPENSE.title: HAND → SUSPENSE Suspense & Questions
M02-5-HAND-SUSPENSE.studentContent:
HAND → SUSPENSE Suspense & Questions Spark curiosity
Question Question Hook
Open with questions to create an information gap
"Did you know 90% of people make this mistake?" "Why does XX always...?"
Reveal Reveal & Twist
Set up suspense then deliver the reveal
"The answer will surprise you..." "What happened next blew my mind"
💡 Best use: SUSPENSE is the ultimate weapon for writing HOOKs. Open with suspense to make the viewer feel they "have to keep watching."
M02-5-HAND-SUSPENSE.notes: SUSPENSE has 2 L2 tactics: Question/Reveal. The core idea is 'creating curiosity.'

M02-5-HAND-URGENCY.title: HAND → URGENCY Scarcity & Urgency
M02-5-HAND-URGENCY.studentContent:
HAND → URGENCY Scarcity & Urgency Drive immediate action
Time Flash Sale
Time-limited offers, countdown promotions
"Limited offer — only 3 hours left" "Sale ends soon"
Stock Limited Stock
Limited quantity, while supplies last
"Only 200 units left" "While supplies last"
💡 Best use: URGENCY is the go-to closer for CTA. After the viewer is persuaded, use urgency to push them to take the final action.
M02-5-HAND-URGENCY.notes: URGENCY has 2 L2 tactics: Time/Stock. The core idea is 'act now.'

M02-5-section-strategy.title: 🔗 Section × Strategy Pairing
M02-5-section-strategy.studentContent:
Each Section has an ideal Strategy combination -- pick the right one and you'll get twice the results with half the effort
Section Psychological Task Recommended L0 Common L1 Combinations
HOOK Capture attention HAND / HEART SUSPENSE(Question/Reveal) · PAIN(Scene) · CONTRAST(AB/Shocking)
BRIDGE Build relevance HEART / HEAD PAIN(Scene/Daily) · EXPLAIN(Logic)
PROBLEM Build tension HEART PAIN(Scene/Daily/Loss) · FEAR(Consequence)
RESOLUTION Release tension HEAD / EYES EXPLAIN(Logic/Data) · DEMO(Step/Multi_Angle)
REVERSAL Shift perception HEAD CONTRAST(AB/Shocking) · STORY(Origin/User)
VALUE Stabilize decision HEAD / EYES PROOF(UGC/Before_After/Sales_Data) · AUTHORITY(Expert/Certification) · DEMO(Step/Multi_Angle)
CTA Drive action HAND URGENCY(Time/Stock) · PROOF(Sales_Data) · FEAR(Miss_Out)
💡 Memory tip: HOOK uses suspense to grab, PROBLEM uses pain points to build tension, RESOLUTION uses logic/demos to release, VALUE uses evidence to stabilize, CTA uses urgency to drive action
M02-5-section-strategy.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
This table is the pairing guide for Section and Strategy.

For example, HOOK needs to capture attention, so HAND's suspense strategy works best;
PROBLEM needs to build tension, so HEART's pain points and fear work best;
CTA needs to drive action, so HAND's urgency works best.

Once you memorize these pairings, you'll have clear direction when writing scripts.
M02-5-section-strategy.notes: Section x Strategy pairing is the core of practical application -- it tells students which strategy to use for each section.

M02-part6-title.title: Part 6
M02-part6-title.subtitle: HOOK Deep-Dive Training
M02-part6-title.notes: HOOK is the biggest lever for viral hits. This is the climax of the module.

M02-6-why.title: Why Is HOOK the Most Important?
M02-6-why.studentContent:
On short-video platforms, the average user attention span is only 3 seconds
If you don't grab the user in the first 3 seconds
Nothing else you do matters
HOOK determines whether a video even gets "seen"
M02-6-why.presenterContent:
📢 Presenter Script
HOOK is the single most important part of any video. Why? Because it determines whether the video gets seen at all.

On platforms like TikTok and Xiaohongshu, the user's thumb is always on the screen, ready to swipe away at any moment. You have exactly 3 seconds to make them stop.
M02-6-why.notes: Emphasize the importance of HOOK, setting the stage for the training ahead.

M02-6-types.title: 5 Ways to Write a HOOK
M02-6-types.studentContent:
1️⃣ Question Type (SUSPENSE-Question)

"Have you ever experienced this..."
"Why does your skin always..."

2️⃣ Number Type (EXPLAIN-Data)

"3 ways to..."
"90% of people don't know..."

3️⃣ Counter-Intuitive Type (CONTRAST-Shocking)

"Never buy this product, unless..."
"The secret that doctors use..."

4️⃣ Suspense Type (SUSPENSE-Reveal)

"What happened next blew my mind..."
"The last method is a game changer..."

5️⃣ Pain Point Type (PAIN-Scene)

"Night owls, watch this!" "Oily skin savior!" "Sensitive skin miracle!"
M02-6-types.presenterContent: 💡 Tip: Each HOOK writing style maps to a specific Strategy L2. The same product can use different types of HOOKs.
M02-6-types.notes: Students should be able to apply all 5 styles flexibly. Note the correspondence with Strategy L2.

M02-6-practice.title: HOOK Writing Workshop
M02-6-practice.studentContent:
🤝 Interactive Session - 8 min
Exercise:
1. Choose a product (you may use your own brand's product)
2. First define the Objective (what is this video trying to achieve?)
3. Write one version of the HOOK using each of the 5 styles
4. Label each HOOK with the Strategy used (L0-L1-L2)
Grading Criteria:
✓ Stopping power: Can it make someone stop scrolling in the first 3 seconds?
✓ Strategy clarity: Can you clearly identify the Strategy used?
✓ Objective alignment: Does the HOOK serve the video's goal?
M02-6-practice.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Action: Walk around the room, note outstanding examples for review. Emphasize the decision chain: Objective → Strategy → HOOK.
M02-6-practice.notes: This is the most important hands-on exercise of the module. Emphasize the complete decision chain.

M02-part7-title.title: Part 7
M02-part7-title.subtitle: Case Study & Summary
M02-part7-title.notes: Use real cases to tie together all the knowledge points.

M02-7-case.title: Case Analysis Template
M02-7-case.studentContent:
Section State Transition Timestamp L0 L1
HOOK Browsing → Attention 0:00-0:03 HAND SUSPENSE
PROBLEM Calm → Uneasy 0:03-0:08 HEART PAIN
RESOLUTION Uneasy → Hopeful 0:08-0:15 HEAD EXPLAIN
VALUE Interested → Persuaded 0:15-0:25 EYES DEMO
CTA Persuaded → Action 0:25-0:30 HAND URGENCY
Example Video: Objective = CONVERT (Immediate Conversion) | Skeleton = HPRVC (Standard 5-Section)
M02-7-case.presenterContent: ▶ Presenter Action: Play 2-3 real video examples and analyze them in real time using this template.
M02-7-case.notes: Demonstrate the analysis approach so students know how to use the Bone system to analyze videos.

M02-7-case-video.title: Case Study Videos
M02-7-case-video.notes: 4 HOOK case study videos, use with the analysis template from the previous slide.

M02-7-quiz.title: 🎯 Knowledge Check
M02-7-quiz.notes: Test whether students understand the complete decision chain.
M02-7-quiz.question: What is the correct decision-making sequence for a video?
M02-7-quiz.explanation: The correct order is: First define the Objective (goal) → Then choose the Skeleton (structure) → Finally assign Strategy (tactics). The goal determines the structure, and the structure determines the strategy.

M02-7-summary.title: Module Summary
M02-7-summary.studentContent:
1️⃣
Objective: 9 video goals -- first clarify what you want to achieve
2️⃣
Section: 7 psychological progression segments -- the building blocks of the skeleton
3️⃣
Skeleton: 8 skeleton presets (Tier A/B/C: Tier-A basic 4-segment / Tier-B 5-segment / Tier-C with Bridge)
4️⃣
Strategy: L0(4) → L1(10) → L2(26) three-tier strategy system
5️⃣
Decision Chain: Objective → Skeleton → Strategy → Content
M02-7-summary.notes: Quick review of the 5 core knowledge points.

M02-end.title: Module 02 Complete ✓
M02-end.subtitle: After the break: Module 03 Meat System
M02-end.notes: M02 is finished. This is the most information-dense module. Let students rest and digest the content.
